source_files.title: 三草酸合铁(III)酸钾的制备 再解读 / Synthesis of Potassium Tris(oxalato) Ferrate(III) - further discussion

source_files.filename: 三草酸合铁酸钾的制备及性质—完整.ppt
source_files.author: 胡锴 (Hu Kai), Laboratory Chemistry Education Center
source_files.title: 三草酸合铁(III)酸钾的制备及其性质 / Synthesis and Characterization of Potassium Tris(oxalato) Ferrate(III)
Oxalate Stoichiometry - Student Written Instructions / Instructor Notes

step1_precipitation.notes: Precipitation of ferrous oxalate dihydrate from Mohr's salt

step2_oxidation_reduction_and_coordinati.notes: Ideal balanced equation for the full conversion

step2_oxidation_with_side_product.notes: When H2O2 is excess, Fe(OH)3 precipitates as a brown side-product

step2_redissolution_of_hydroxide.notes: Subsequent addition of oxalic acid dissolves the Fe(OH)3 precipitate

alternative_route1_fe3_direct.notes: Direct preparation from Fe(III). PROBLEM: Fe3+ can undergo redox side reaction with C2O42- (reduction to Fe2+ + CO2). Route 2 (via Fe2+ → FeC2O4) is preferred.

alternative_route2_solid_phase.notes: Can be done by solution method or solid-phase synthesis with controlled conditions

doc_fecl2_synthesis.source: DOC file (Prelab answer key for desk 1,4,7,...)

doc_ni_pyrolysis_options.A: 2NiC2O4·2H2O(s) + O2(g) → 2NiO(s) + 4H2O(g) + 4CO2(g) [0.41 g from 1.0 g - ACTUALLY OCCURS, black NiO]
doc_ni_pyrolysis_options.C: NiC2O4·2H2O(s) → NiCO3(s) + 2H2O(g) + CO(g) [0.65 g from 1.0 g]

doc_mn_pyrolysis_options.B: 3MnC2O4·3H2O(s) + 2O2(g) → Mn3O4(s) + 9H2O(g) + 6CO2(g) [0.39 g from 1.0 g - ACTUALLY OCCURS, brown-black Mn3O4]
doc_mn_pyrolysis_options.C: MnC2O4·3H2O(s) → MnCO3(s) + 3H2O(g) + CO(g) [0.58 g from 1.0 g]

feC2O4_2H2O_vacuum_dehydration.description: FeC2O4·2H2O loses crystal water under vacuum

K3FeC2O43_3H2O_dehydration.description: K3[Fe(C2O4)3]·3H2O loses all 3 crystal water molecules
K3FeC2O43_3H2O_dehydration.comparison_with_faq: FAQ states 干燥 110°C. Literature states ~100°C. Slight discrepancy (~10°C).

K3FeC2O43_3H2O_decomposition.comparison_with_faq: FAQ states 分解 230-250°C. Literature states 230°C (onset). Consistent with lower bound.

water_bath_h2o2_addition.description: Water bath temperature for dropwise H2O2 addition during oxidation step

water_bath_oxalic_acid_addition.description: Water bath temperature for H2C2O4 addition to dissolve Fe(OH)3 and complete coordination

doc_pyrolysis_initial.description: Hot plate setting for pyrolysis of metal oxalates (~400°C)

doc_pyrolysis_secondary.description: Increased temperature if constant mass not achieved at 400°C

doc_max_hotplate.description: Maximum hot plate temperature (Fisher Scientific Isotemp)

boiling_to_remove_h2o2.description: Solution heated to boiling after H2O2 addition to decompose excess H2O2, then maintained at 85°C water bath

mohr_salt.notes: Plus a few drops of H2SO4

ppt_procedure.notes: K2C2O4 is added BEFORE H2O2 oxidation, not after

doc_procedure.oxalic_acid_solution_prep: 112 g H2C2O4·2H2O per 1 L distilled water

FeC2O4_2H2O.note: Sparingly soluble. Hot water solubility is LOWER than cold water.

K3_Fe_C2O4_3_3H2O.note: This solubility difference enables the solvent replacement crystallization method

mohr_salt.solubility: soluble in water, poorly soluble in ethanol
mohr_salt.solubility_vs_FeSO4: less soluble in water than FeSO4
mohr_salt.uses: reducing agent, primary standard for K2Cr2O7 and KMnO4

oxalic_acid.acid_strength_note: ~10,000 times stronger than acetic acid at same concentration
oxalic_acid.ligand_type: bidentate, intermediate field, strong reducing agent
oxalic_acid.decomposition: thermally unstable, decomposes to CO2 upon heating

fe_limiting_reactant_factor.description: For FeCl2·4H2O → FeC2O4·2H2O: multiply mass of metal salt used by 0.9048 to get theoretical yield (4 significant figures)

step1_FeC2O4_preparation.reagents: 5.0 g (NH4)2SO4·FeSO4·6H2O + few drops H2SO4; 1.7 g H2C2O4·2H2O
step1_FeC2O4_preparation.method: Dissolve reactants separately, mix, heat to boiling with anti-bumping measures, microboil 4 min
step1_FeC2O4_preparation.washing: Hot water wash by decantation (倾滗法), test for SO42- absence
key_questions: Why add acid to Mohr's salt solution?
Why does bumping occur and how to prevent it?
Why use SO42- as indicator of washing completeness?

step2_oxidation_coordination.reagents: 3.5 g K2C2O4 + 10 mL H2O; 6% H2O2 8mL; H2C2O4 (until clear emerald green)
procedure: Add K2C2O4 solution to FeC2O4·2H2O precipitate (BEFORE H2O2)
Fe(OH)3 brown precipitate forms (side product from excess H2O2)
Heat to boiling to decompose excess H2O2
85°C water bath, add H2C2O4 until solution turns clear emerald green (翠绿色)
Control H2C2O4 amount carefully - stop exactly when transparent green
step2_oxidation_coordination.why_boil_before_oxalic: To remove excess H2O2 which would oxidize C2O42-
step2_oxidation_coordination.why_stop_at_emerald_green: Excess H2C2O4 causes side reactions; insufficient leaves uncomplexed Fe3+

step3_crystallization.principle: Exploits solubility difference in different solvents to precipitate target compound
step3_crystallization.detail: Add ethanol to aqueous solution; K3[Fe(C2O4)3]·3H2O is insoluble in ethanol
step3_crystallization.cotton_thread: Provides nucleation/growth attachment point for crystals
references: 燕翔, 制备硫酸亚铁铵单晶的实验研究, 首都师范大学学报, 2015,36(4):49-52
三草酸合铁酸钾制备条件的优化, 台州学院学报, 2009, 31(3):49-51

doc_part1_synthesis.description: General oxalate precipitation: 3.9-4.1 g hydrated metal salt + 20 mL water, add to 50.0 mL 0.888 M H2C2O4 (4.0 g H2C2O4), wait 10 min, vacuum filter, wash with acetone (3 × 5-10 mL)
doc_part1_synthesis.note: Metal salt is the limiting reactant for all three metals (Fe, Ni, Mn)

doc_part2_pyrolysis.description: Weigh 0.990-1.010 g dry hydrated metal oxalate in aluminum dish, heat on hot plate at ~400°C, cool, reweigh. Repeat 10-min cycles until mass change <0.010 g. Increase to 450°C if needed.
doc_part2_pyrolysis.fe_result: Reaction B: product is Fe2O3 (red-brown/rust). NOT FeO, NOT FeCO3.
doc_part2_pyrolysis.ni_result: Reaction A: product is NiO (black). Do NOT stop at yellow intermediate.
doc_part2_pyrolysis.mn_result: Reaction B: product is Mn3O4 (brown-black), actually mixture with Mn2O3.

photochemical_activity.description: K3[Fe(C2O4)3]·3H2O has photochemical activity - absorbs light energy and quantitatively undergoes photochemical reaction
applications: Photosensitive paper (感光纸) for photographic experiments
Chemical actinometer (化学光量计) for measuring light quantity and photodegradation
photochemical_activity.mechanism_hint: Light-triggered reaction, decomposes upon light exposure
photochemical_activity.comparison_with_faq: FAQ mentions LMCT ~530nm. Neither PPT nor PPTX specify the exact wavelength, but both confirm photochemical activity.

catalytic_applications: Main raw material for preparing supported active iron catalysts
Good catalyst for some organic reactions

coordination_chemistry.ligand_field: C2O42- is a bidentate intermediate-field ligand
coordination_chemistry.stability_comparison: Fe3+ complexes: compare SCN-, F-, C2O42-, CN-
coordination_chemistry.charge_determination: Ion exchange method + conductivity method for determining complex ion charge

mohr_salt_stability_theories.void_theory: Surface void size determines probability of O2 entry and oxidation
mohr_salt_stability_theories.hydrogen_bond_theory: NH4+ and SO42- embed in crystal lattice forming more hydrogen bonds, increasing stability
references: 贾艳媛等, 同铁不同性: FeSO4·7H2O和(NH4)2Fe(SO4)2·6H2O——硫酸亚铁铵制备的改进实验, 大学化学, 2023, 38(11):155-162
李俊生等, 硫酸亚铁铵和硫酸亚铁稳定性的对比研究, 化学教育, 2014, 9:69-74

biological_medical_context.kidney_stones: Calcium oxalate is most common; heaviest recorded 620 g (Pakistan, 2008); pain threshold ~2-3 mm diameter
biological_medical_context.autism_research: Preliminary: autistic children have high urinary oxalates; low-oxalate diet as treatment
biological_medical_context.iron_oxalate: FeC2O4 (humboldtine) - yellow pigment in paints/glass/plastic; evidence for life on other planets (fungi/lichen product)
biological_medical_context.nanoparticle_precursors: Transition metal oxalates as precursors to metal oxide nanoparticles for catalysis and semiconductors

drying_temperature.discrepancy: ~10°C. Literature uses ~ as approximation. Both refer to loss of 3H2O crystal water.
drying_temperature.assessment: Minor discrepancy; literature is approximate. 110°C is a reasonable practical drying temperature to ensure complete dehydration.

decomposition_temperature.assessment: Literature gives onset at 230°C. FAQ range 230-250°C is consistent, implying decomposition proceeds over a range.

lmct_wavelength.literature_value: NOT specified in any of the three files
lmct_wavelength.assessment: The PPT and PPTX mention photochemical activity and use as chemical actinometer but do not specify LMCT wavelength. This wavelength data must come from other sources.

feC2O4_solubility_anomaly.finding: FeC2O4·2H2O solubility in hot water (0.026g/100g) is LOWER than in cold water (0.22g/100g)
feC2O4_solubility_anomaly.significance: This is an important experimental detail - hot water washing actually helps retain the precipitate

new_data_not_in_typical_faq.pyrolysis_products: FeC2O4·2H2O pyrolyzes to Fe2O3 (not FeO or FeCO3) in air at ~400°C
new_data_not_in_typical_faq.stoichiometric_factor: For FeCl2·4H2O → FeC2O4·2H2O: theoretical yield = metal salt mass × 0.9048
new_data_not_in_typical_faq.K2C2O4_addition_order: ADD BEFORE H2O2 addition, not after. This is critical for successful synthesis.
new_data_not_in_typical_faq.side_reaction_mechanism: Excess H2O2 produces Fe(OH)3 brown precipitate; this is redissolved by subsequent H2C2O4 addition

from_pptx: 贾艳媛等, 大学化学, 2023, 38(11):155-162 (Mohr's salt stability)
李俊生等, 化学教育, 2014, 9:69-74 (FeSO4 vs Mohr's salt stability)
燕翔, 首都师范大学学报, 2015,36(4):49-52 (Solvent replacement crystallization)
台州学院学报, 2009, 31(3):49-51 (Optimization of K3[Fe(C2O4)3] preparation)
from_ppt: Same refs as PPTX for Mohr's salt and crystallization

summary_statistics.total_pages_doc: ~14 pages (student instructions + instructor notes + answer keys)
